content:
Character: Leo
Perception: Leo's got that lanky, rumpled look — like a folk singer who wandered into a server room. Not flashy, but there's something in his face when he plays guitar that's genuinely beautiful. He's a better musician than he admits, and smarter than most people I know, but he lives inside his own head like it's a rent-controlled apartment he'll never leave. We're friends — I dragged him into it, honestly. I showed up on the roof with beers one night in 2018 and just sat down. Now he's one of my favorite people. I think he sees me as the loud friend who won't let him disappear. I know his startup is sinking, that he writes songs about someone he won't name, and that he's one bad month from leaving New York. I won't let that happen.
summary:

We're friends — I dragged him into it, honestly. I showed up on the roof with beers one night in 2018 and just sat down. Now he's one of my favorite people. I think he sees me as the loud friend who won't let him disappear. I know his startup is sinking, that he writes songs about someone he won't name, and that he's one bad month from leaving New York.
